summary: Re-tested WWEmbed (Next.js + FastAPI proxy + MongoDB) end-to-end. Backend pytest: 32/32 passing (14 original + 18 new in test_wwembed_extra.py covering forgot-password, reset-password, all embed v1 routes with correct prefixes, /api/v1/stats, /api/admin/stats, /api/openapi, frontend-page no-5xx checks). Frontend e2e via Playwright: homepage + /auth/login + /auth/sign-up + /auth/forgot-password render correctly; the iteration_1 critical bug (login → /dashboard middleware redirect loop) is RESOLVED — admin login now lands on /dashboard with ww_access/ww_refresh cookies, /admin shows full admin dashboard, /docs Swagger UI loads, /profile/admin works, /embed/{wwId}/stats works, logout clears cookies, fresh sign-up creates user + lands on /dashboard with role=member.

issues: Console emits a TypeError from @swagger-api/apidom-ns-openapi-3-1 turbopack chunk on first load. UI still renders all routes correctly — non-blocking, but worth investigating to clean up dev console.

action_items: (Optional) Investigate the swagger-api/apidom TypeError on /docs — UI still works, but the console error is noisy.

(Clarify with PM) Review request lists '/profile' and '/embed/[wwId]' as expected pages, but the actual implementation only exposes '/profile/[username]' and '/embed/[wwId]/stats' (no '/embed/[wwId]/page.tsx'). If a bare /embed/{wwId} HTML view is desired, a page.tsx must be added; otherwise update the spec/UX to point users to the existing routes.
(Hardening, low priority) ww_access / ww_refresh cookies are HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax but missing the Secure flag on this HTTPS-only site.
critical_code_review_comments: Iteration_1 bugs are fixed: (a) FastAPI proxy now preserves multi Set-Cookie headers, and (b) Next.js login redirect to /dashboard now works — middleware accepts the ww_access JWT.

Each embed v1 route enforces a different wwId prefix (ww-movie-/ww-tv- for streaming/download/links, ww-live- for live, ww-ebook- for ebook, ww-music- for music, ww-software-/ww-game- for digital). This is consistent and well-validated; tests now cover all six endpoints.
TMDB integration (/api/search, /api/media/movie/{id}) returns valid data with the configured TMDB_API_KEY.
/api/db RBAC correctly returns 200 for live_tv_channels and 401/403 for users — no leak of the users table to anonymous clients.
/api/admin/stats correctly returns 401 when unauthenticated and 200 with admin session — properly guarded.
/api/auth/forgot-password returns ok=true for both valid and unknown emails — anti-enumeration is correctly implemented.
/api/auth/reset-password returns 400/401 for an invalid token — token validation is enforced.
test_credentials: admin@wwembed.test / admin1234 (admin); fresh sign-ups via /auth/sign-up create role=member users.
seed_data_creation: None. Each register/sign-up test uses a uuid- or timestamp-suffixed unique email; admin user already seeded.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend = green (32/32 pytest). Frontend = green on auth flows, dashboard, admin, /docs, /profile/{username}, /embed/{wwId}/stats. Two 'expected' 404s are by design: /profile (bare, without username) and /embed/{wwId} (no page.tsx — only /stats subroute is implemented). If product wants those, main agent must add page.tsx. Otherwise no action items remain.
rca of the issue: All P0 bugs from iteration_1 are fixed. (1) Set-Cookie collapse: previously fixed by switching httpx headers to multi_items()/raw_headers in /app/backend/server.py. Verified: Playwright sees both ww_access and ww_refresh cookies in the jar after login. (2) Login → /dashboard redirect: previously the Next.js middleware was rejecting valid JWT cookies; this is now consistent — POST /api/auth/login → page navigates straight to /dashboard, /admin and /profile/{username} all render correctly with the same session.
